entry.title: Re: I have a business requirement in SAP EWM
entry.content: Hi Ajit, We have LOSC setup and also WOCR

entry.title: Unlocking the Future of Digital Marketing: How AI-Driven Voice Search Is Revolutionizing Business
entry.content:
In the rapidly evolving landscape of digital marketing, few trends have captured the imagination and driven innovation quite like voice search. As we navigate through 2025, it's clear that voice search is no longer just a novelty, it's a full-fledged revolution. With AI at the helm, voice search is transforming how we interact with content, and businesses are racing to adapt. To leverage this trend effectively, marketers can benefit from enrolling in SEO Certificate Programs, which provide comprehensive insights into optimizing for voice search. Additionally, for those in Mumbai, Digital Marketing Training Mumbai courses can offer specialized knowledge on how to tailor content for local voice search queries. For those interested in learning more about SEO in the context of voice search, Learn SEO in Mumbai workshops can provide valuable hands-on experience.

Introduction to Voice Search

Voice search has come a long way since its inception. From its early days as a feature on smartphones to its current ubiquity in smart speakers and virtual assistants, voice search has evolved significantly. Today, it's projected that voice search will account for a substantial portion of all searches, with over 1 billion voice searches conducted each month. This dramatic shift is driven by the increasing sophistication of voice assistants like Google Assistant, Apple’s Siri, and Amazon’s Alexa, along with the growing adoption of smart speakers and smartphones. By the end of 2025, voice search is expected to account for over 50% of all searches, marking a significant milestone in its evolution. Marketers looking to capitalize on this trend should consider enrolling in SEO Certificate Programs to gain a deeper understanding of voice search optimization strategies. For those in Mumbai, Digital Marketing Training Mumbai can help tailor these strategies for local businesses. Furthermore, Learn SEO in Mumbai courses can provide essential insights into how to optimize content for voice search.

The Evolution of Voice Search

Early Days to Current Trends

Voice search began as a feature on smartphones, allowing users to perform searches using voice commands. However, with the advent of smart speakers and virtual assistants, voice search has become ubiquitous. Today, voice assistants are integrated into various devices, from smartphones and smart speakers to cars and home appliances. This integration has made voice search a seamless part of daily life, allowing users to access information, control devices, and perform tasks with ease. For those interested in optimizing for voice search, SEO Certificate Programs can provide valuable insights into how to structure content for these devices. Additionally, Digital Marketing Training Mumbai can help businesses in Mumbai understand how to tailor their content for local voice queries. For those looking to dive deeper into SEO strategies, Learn SEO in Mumbai workshops can offer practical experience in optimizing for voice search.

Impact on Consumer Behavior

Voice search has significantly impacted consumer behavior, particularly in how people interact with content. Voice queries are often more conversational and question-based, which requires businesses to rethink their content strategies. For instance, voice search results tend to come from featured snippets and top-ranking pages, favoring long-form, conversational content. This shift has led to a greater emphasis on creating content that answers questions directly and naturally. Marketers can benefit from SEO Certificate Programs to learn how to craft such content effectively. In Mumbai, Digital Marketing Training Mumbai can help businesses adapt their content strategies for local voice search. Furthermore, Learn SEO in Mumbai courses can provide detailed guidance on optimizing content for voice search.

Statistical Overview

  • Adoption Rates: The number of voice assistant users in the U.S. is expected to reach 162.7 million by 2025.
  • Usage Patterns: 76% of smart speaker users perform local voice searches at least weekly.
  • Satisfaction Levels: 93% of consumers are satisfied with their voice assistants, indicating high trust and engagement.

To better understand these statistics and how they impact local businesses, SEO Certificate Programs can offer comprehensive insights into voice search trends. For those in Mumbai, Digital Marketing Training Mumbai can provide strategies tailored to the local market. Additionally, Learn SEO in Mumbai workshops can help marketers analyze and leverage these statistics effectively.

Latest Features, Tools, and Trends

AI Integration in Voice Search

AI is at the heart of voice search's evolution. By leveraging AI, voice assistants can better understand natural language, offer more accurate results, and even predict user intent. This integration has significantly improved the user experience, making voice search more intuitive and efficient. AI-driven voice assistants can now handle complex queries and provide personalized responses, enhancing the overall experience for users. For marketers seeking to optimize for these AI-driven voice assistants, SEO Certificate Programs can provide essential training on AI integration in voice search. In Mumbai, Digital Marketing Training Mumbai courses can help businesses understand how AI impacts local voice search strategies. Furthermore, Learn SEO in Mumbai initiatives can offer practical insights into leveraging AI for voice search optimization.

Voice Search Optimization Tools

  • SEO Auditing Tools: Platforms like Ahrefs and SEMrush now include features specifically designed to analyze and improve voice search rankings. These tools help identify gaps in content and provide insights into how to better optimize for voice queries.
  • Content Optimization Tools: Tools like Content Blossom help create content that is more conversational and optimized for voice queries. This includes crafting content that answers questions directly and naturally.
  • Structured Data and Schema Markup: Essential for helping voice assistants understand and feature your content, schema markup has become a crucial element in voice search optimization. By using schema markup, businesses can ensure that their content is highlighted in voice search results.

For marketers looking to utilize these tools effectively, SEO Certificate Programs can provide comprehensive training on how to integrate them into their voice search strategies. In Mumbai, Digital Marketing Training Mumbai can help businesses choose the right tools for local voice search optimization. Additionally, Learn SEO in Mumbai workshops can offer hands-on experience with these tools.

Voice Commerce and Its Impact

Voice commerce is another area where AI-driven voice search is making a significant impact. With voice commerce projected to reach $80 billion annually by 2025, businesses are leveraging AI to enhance the shopping experience. This includes using AI to personalize product recommendations, streamline checkout processes, and improve customer service. Voice commerce allows customers to shop hands-free, making it a convenient option for busy consumers. For those interested in optimizing for voice commerce, SEO Certificate Programs can offer insights into how AI enhances the shopping experience. In Mumbai, Digital Marketing Training Mumbai can help businesses understand how to integrate voice commerce into their local strategies. Furthermore, strong>Learn SEO in Mumbai initiatives can provide practical guidance on optimizing for voice commerce.

Advanced Tactics for Success

Conversational Content Creation

One of the most effective strategies for optimizing content for voice search is to create conversational, question-based content. Voice queries are often phrased as questions, so crafting content that answers these questions directly can significantly improve visibility in voice search results. This involves using natural language and focusing on long-form content that provides comprehensive answers. For marketers seeking to create such content, SEO Certificate Programs can provide valuable training on conversational content creation. In Mumbai, Digital Marketing Training Mumbai courses can help businesses tailor their content for local voice search queries. Additionally, Learn SEO in Mumbai workshops can offer practical insights into crafting conversational content.

Local SEO and Voice Search

Local businesses see the greatest impact from voice search optimization. With 58% of consumers using voice to find local information, businesses must ensure their local SEO is robust. This includes optimizing Google My Business listings, using location-specific keywords, and ensuring accurate NAP (Name, Address, Phone Number) consistency across the web. For those in Mumbai, Digital Marketing Training Mumbai can help businesses optimize their local SEO strategies for voice search. Additionally, Learn SEO in Mumbai initiatives can provide detailed guidance on local SEO optimization. Marketers can also benefit from SEO Certificate Programs to gain a comprehensive understanding of local SEO strategies.

Mobile Optimization

Since the majority of voice searches occur on smartphones, mobile optimization is no longer optional. Ensuring that your website is mobile-friendly, loads quickly, and provides a seamless user experience is crucial for success in voice search. This includes optimizing images, minimizing page load times, and ensuring that all content is easily accessible on mobile devices. For marketers seeking to optimize their websites for mobile, SEO Certificate Programs can provide essential training on mobile optimization techniques. In Mumbai, Digital Marketing Training Mumbai can help businesses ensure their websites are mobile-friendly for local voice search. Furthermore, Learn SEO in Mumbai workshops can offer practical insights into mobile optimization strategies.

Storytelling and Community Engagement

Content remains the backbone of any successful SEO strategy, including voice search. By focusing on storytelling and community engagement, businesses can create a deeper connection with their audience. This can be achieved by:

  • Storytelling: Using narratives to make your content more relatable and engaging. This can help increase engagement and brand loyalty.
  • Community Engagement: Encouraging user-generated content and fostering discussions around your brand. This not only builds trust but also provides valuable insights into what your audience is looking for.

For marketers interested in leveraging storytelling and community engagement, SEO Certificate Programs can offer insights into how these strategies enhance voice search visibility. In Mumbai, Digital Marketing Training Mumbai can help businesses integrate storytelling into their local voice search strategies. Additionally, Learn SEO in Mumbai initiatives can provide practical guidance on community engagement for voice search optimization.

Influencer Partnerships and User-Generated Content

Influencer partnerships and user-generated content can play a significant role in enhancing voice search visibility. By partnering with influencers who have a strong voice search presence, businesses can tap into their audience and increase brand awareness. User-generated content, meanwhile, provides social proof and can help improve local SEO by showcasing real customer experiences. For marketers seeking to leverage influencer partnerships, SEO Certificate Programs can provide training on how to integrate these partnerships into voice search strategies. In Mumbai, Digital Marketing Training Mumbai can help businesses find influencers with a strong local presence. Furthermore, Learn SEO in Mumbai workshops can offer practical insights into using user-generated content for local voice search optimization.

Measuring Success: Analytics and Insights

Measuring the success of voice search strategies requires a nuanced approach. Traditional metrics like website traffic and engagement metrics are still relevant, but businesses must also track voice-specific metrics such as:

  • Voice Search Conversions: Monitor how many users complete a desired action (e.g., making a purchase or filling out a form) after using voice search.
  • Voice Assistant Engagement: Track how users interact with voice assistants to access your content.

Tools like Google Analytics can be adapted to track these metrics, providing valuable insights into the effectiveness of your voice search strategies. For marketers seeking to measure the success of their voice search efforts, SEO Certificate Programs can offer comprehensive training on analytics and insights. In Mumbai, Digital Marketing Training Mumbai can help businesses set up analytics for local voice search metrics. Additionally, Learn SEO in Mumbai initiatives can provide practical guidance on interpreting voice search analytics.

Business Case Study: Domino's Pizza

Dominos Pizza is a prime example of a brand that has successfully leveraged AI-driven voice search strategies. By integrating with voice assistants like Alexa and Google Assistant, Domino's allows customers to order pizzas using voice commands. This not only enhances customer convenience but also drives sales through voice commerce. Domino's faced the challenge of ensuring that their voice ordering system was seamless and user-friendly. They worked closely with AI developers to refine their voice ordering process, ensuring that it could handle a wide range of voice commands and accents. The results were impressive. Domino's saw a significant increase in orders placed through voice assistants, with customers appreciating the convenience and speed of ordering pizzas using voice commands. This strategy not only improved customer satisfaction but also positioned Domino's as a leader in voice commerce. For marketers interested in replicating this success, SEO Certificate Programs can provide insights into how to integrate AI-driven voice search into their strategies. In Mumbai, Digital Marketing Training Mumbai can help businesses understand how to apply similar strategies to local voice commerce. Additionally, Learn SEO in Mumbai workshops can offer practical guidance on optimizing for voice commerce.

Additional Case Studies

To further illustrate the impact of voice search, let's consider a few more case studies:

  • Amazon: Amazon's Alexa has been instrumental in promoting voice commerce. By allowing users to shop using voice commands, Amazon has seen significant growth in voice-driven sales.
  • Walmart: Walmart has integrated voice shopping into its app, allowing customers to order groceries and other items using voice assistants. This has improved customer convenience and increased sales.

For marketers seeking to learn from these case studies, SEO Certificate Programs can offer insights into how to apply similar strategies to their own businesses. In Mumbai, Digital Marketing Training Mumbai can help businesses analyze these case studies in the context of local voice search. Furthermore, Learn SEO in Mumbai initiatives can provide practical guidance on how to integrate voice commerce into local strategies.

Future Developments in Voice Search

As voice search continues to evolve, several future developments are worth noting:

  • Advancements in AI: Future advancements in AI will likely improve the accuracy and personalization of voice assistants, making them even more intuitive and user-friendly.
  • New Devices and Integrations: The integration of voice assistants into more devices, such as cars and home appliances, will continue to expand the reach of voice search.
  • Privacy Concerns: As voice search becomes more ubiquitous, privacy concerns will become increasingly important. Businesses will need to ensure that user data is protected while still providing personalized experiences.

For marketers interested in staying ahead of these developments, SEO Certificate Programs can provide comprehensive training on future trends in voice search. In Mumbai, Digital Marketing Training Mumbai can help businesses prepare for these future developments in the local market. Additionally, Learn SEO in Mumbai workshops can offer practical insights into how to adapt to future voice search technologies.

Challenges in Implementing Voice Search Strategies

Voice search offers numerous opportunities, but businesses also face several challenges when implementing voice search strategies:

  • Technical Challenges: Ensuring that voice search systems are technically sound and can handle a wide range of queries and accents is crucial.
  • Content Optimization: Creating content that is optimized for voice search requires a significant shift in content strategy, which can be challenging for some businesses.
  • User Adoption: Encouraging users to adopt voice search can be difficult, especially among older demographics.

Solutions to Common Challenges

  • Collaboration with AI Developers: Working closely with AI developers can help refine voice search systems and ensure they are user-friendly.
  • Content Strategy Overhaul: Businesses should be willing to overhaul their content strategy to focus on conversational, question-based content.
  • User Education: Educating users about the benefits of voice search can encourage adoption and increase usage.

For marketers seeking to overcome these challenges, SEO Certificate Programs can offer insights into how to address technical and content-related issues. In Mumbai, Digital Marketing Training Mumbai can help businesses develop strategies to encourage user adoption of voice search. Additionally, Learn SEO in Mumbai initiatives can provide practical guidance on content strategy overhauls for voice search optimization.

Actionable Tips for Marketers

As you navigate the world of AI-driven voice search, here are some actionable tips to help you succeed:

  1. Optimize for Conversational Queries: Craft content that answers questions directly, using natural language.
  2. Use Structured Data: Ensure your website includes schema markup to help voice assistants understand your content.
  3. Focus on Local SEO: If you're a local business, prioritize optimizing your Google My Business listing and using location-specific keywords.

entry.title: Re: Create new DME format under Format Tree UMS1 - S4Hana...
entry.content:
Hi Jeremy,

Thanks for your contribution and confirming our case.

The issue is that I cannot find the existing Format Tree 'CZ_EVAT_XML', which is the only one under Tree type 'UMS1'. Therefore, I cannot copy i,t and I found no way to create anything under the Tree type UMS1.

The Fiori 'Map Format Data' does not include the UMS1 tree type at all. Do you know if it would be anywhere else?

Kind regards,
Evros

entry.title: Re: How to Enable Auto-Approval for Leaves of Three Days...
entry.content: Hi Gurdeep, I think it is feasible. In the business rule need to add one more condition in all the blocks to validate the count of request days.

entry.title: Re: ME21N Confirmation Control Key
entry.content: I solved this problem. If you checked the areas I checked and didn't find the solution you should delete confirmation control key in number of purchasing info record.

entry.title: Re: I have a business requirement in SAP EWM
entry.content: "We have LOSC setup and also WOCR" -> Please change to POSC since you have to pack in intermidiate/pack bin

entry.title: Re: Instance creation failed - abap trial
entry.content: @Andre_Fischer , Here is the full error screenshot:

entry.title: lead qualification how to auto set based on date
entry.content: i want to auto set lead qualification as ( hot, cold, warm ), when expected date of sales is with in 30 days as hot , 30 to 60 days as warm , and beyond 60 days cold, . please help me on how to configure in C4C

entry.title: Re: ABAP Cloud Developer Trial 2023 Available Now
entry.content:
I noticed that `hdb.sapHDB_HDB0` continually runs flat out using 99-100% CPU inside the container. I am running under Docker Desktop on Mac, allocated 6 cores and 48GB to docker.

Are others also seeing this usage, and also on windows or linux?

entry.title: Re: Help Needed: How to Get Last Purchase Price of Produc...
entry.content: Have you considered building your own custom report with date filtering for online viewing or OData export. You can do this via Business Analytics > Design Reports from the SAP Main Menu.

entry.title: Re: Business Rules (on object, Save) not being triggered...
entry.content:
Hi @mithilapethe 

In the business rule which you mapped in the event type "OnSave", navigate to the "Details" section and at "Rule Contexts" section can you check the input for "Import"? If it is "no" then while importing this save rule won't be considered. 

To validate the same, try to change the input for "Import" to "yes" then execute the import. Recommended to check this feature in test environment, if it is working as expected then replicate the same in Production.

Thanks

Arun

entry.title: Changing G/L Master Data from Cash Acct. to Balance Sheet Acct.
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

We want to change G/L account master data. Originally we followed the approach to simplify the chart of accounts for bank accounts as described here: How to simplify my Chart of Accounts via reducing ... - SAP Community

For this purpose, we have maintained the following G/L accounts in the Customizing Tenant:

Bank Reconciliation Account

 

Bank sub-account

 

 

Now we want to change the Type. 1st step is adjusting 1101920 to make sure it is no longer referencing 11001900. 2nd we want to change 11001900, however the following error message arises although 11001920 is no longer assigned to 11001900.

 

There are no entries in CBC referencing these accounts, however in the test tenant we used the Accounts while applying the simplification approach. The balance in these accounts is ZERO.

How can we change the G/L Master Data Settings in the customizing tenant?

entry.title: Re: Rework required for Fiori apps exposed in SAP Workzon...
entry.content: Hi Fabio, I'm not a developer. This is just a Fiori App from OpenText for invoice management. But I can imagine some Fiori apps exist that use client-side code for connecting to external data sources (as Content Repositories). If these are offered via SAP Workzone, code must be modified somehow to keep these connections working.

entry.title: Re: Integration flows do not work in the Integration Suit...
entry.content: Thanks for your input. It looks like these issues are common in the SAP Integration Suite Trial. The trial version has several limitations

entry.title: Re: ABAP Error
entry.content: Isn't there a way to contact SAP in the CAL Web page?

entry.title: Re: FREE_SELECTIONS_DIALOG with Field names
entry.content: As there's no other solution proposed in the documentation, I guess it's a limit of the tool...

entry.title: Re: Advanced Intercompany Sales & Stock Transfer in SAP S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
Hi Gordon,

I have aligned your question with our product manager for the supplier invoice.

We are not 100% clear what you mean with 'spliiting the posting' / 'splitting the ICo supplier invoice'.

From our perspective the system should post one supplier invoice for every supplier invoice being received, means one legal invoice must not be split into 2 or more supplier invoices in the system.
Instead of posting one legal invoice as several supplier invoices you should split the customer invoice instead.

Best regards,
Bernd

entry.title: How to change date format to YYYY-MM-DD Story Report
entry.content:
Hi,

There isn't an easy way to change the date format directly in Story Reports. However, SAP provides a KBA that explains how to manually convert the date format:
2986093 - How to manually change the Date Format for a date field in Story report - SAP for Me

I’d also like to share my formula for this, including how to return 0 if the month or day  have one digits.

CONCAT(
TOTEXT(YEAR([FIELDNAME])),
CONCAT("-",
CONCAT(
RIGHT(CONCAT("0", TOTEXT(MONTH([FIELDNAME]))), 2),
CONCAT("-",
RIGHT(CONCAT("0", TOTEXT(DAY([FIELDNAME]))), 2)
)
)
)
)

entry.title: Re: Strategy Group 52 in SAP IBP (Planning w/o Final Asse...
Re: How to develop a custom program to find 'Where Used L...
entry.content: Program RS_ABAP_SOURCE_SCAN can show in which programs/includes the table names are found.

entry.title: Re: Strategy Group 52 in SAP IBP (Planning w/o Final Asse...
entry.content:
Custom development:

  • Use the new oDATA feature of OBP to download pegging results.
  • Write a custom SE38 report that sets the conversion indicator for planned orders based on your oDATA extraction of planned order pegging.
  • Use periodic RTI Outbound integration for planned orders.
  • Use an external scheduler and make sure that the jobs for:
  1. the OBP run,
  2. the oData Download
  3. Job Based RTI Outbound of planned orders
  4. and the custom S/4 report

are scheduled one after the other without a time gap in between.


See also: Extracting Detailed Pegging Data for External Systems | SAP Help Portal / Step-by-Step Guide: Extracting Pegging Data with t... - SAP Community.

entry.title: Re: Taxes not appearing in purchase order
entry.content: Hey Buddy, Check your Access Sequence is maintained correctly or not or You can remove the access sequence in your Taxes Input conditions.

entry.title: Re: How to Create a Pro Forma invoice (F5) for multiple s...
entry.content: Already found it. When selecting More Documents, I can add more

entry.title: What SAP roles are required for an user to access /sap/xi/engine from CPI
entry.content:
Hi Team,

We have been migration our PO interfaces to CPI and we are using Basic authentication to connect with SAP systems.

While migrating proxy interfaces we are facing Authorization issues. The user we are using for all other communications is giving error '401- Unauthorized' when tried to access /sap/xi/engine.

What roles are required here? what options are available?

entry.title: Re: Adding mail text in email - IS-U invoice through Email
entry.content:
Hello users - I know this thread is too old, but I wanted to know if anyone has got a solution for this - How to add a email text(body) while bill print via EA29/EA60? 

Regards, Manish.

entry.title: Re: Use case for People Profile Portlet links in SAP SuccessFactors Performance Forms
entry.content:
Hi @ujjalgupta2 ,

Unfortunately, this is not working, or at least not as conveniently as it was before. You can still configure a link, but not with this preview as you could with the block links before. (without creating new tab)

Block links configured before enabling the latest profile are still working. A workaround would be to revert to the legacy version, take the IDs, and then revert to the latest profile. Overall, it's not very admin/user-friendly.

entry.title: Re: Technical Error in PCC
entry.content:
Hi Swapna,

What type of policy is being added? (e.g., master data checks, payroll results checks) and Was any recent change made to policy templates, PCC customizing, or OData services?

Please check this KBA: 3129979 - PCC Sorry

entry.title: Re: Extend SAP SuccessFactors on SAP BTP with CAP - Add Business Logic
entry.content:
Hello Alessandro,

Thank you for sharing this series with us.

I’m currently facing an issue with the SFSF user data. Although the connection to the SuccessFactors API has been successfully established, I'm not receiving any records for that object.
{
"@odata.context": "$metadata#SFSF_User",
"value": []
}
Do you know what might be causing this or what steps I should take to resolve it?

entry.title: Atualização data rejeição NFe em Homologação
entry.content:
Após muita confusão e dúvidas sobre a partir de qual data as NF-e emitidas em homologação sem as tags da Reforma Tributária serão rejeitadas, o Governo se pronunciou e publicou a atualização da NT 2025.002 v 1.10, destacando a data de 06/10/2025 para rejeição dos documentos sem as tags da reforma tributária no ambiente de homologação.
Essa atualização trará um alívio nos projetos, uma vez que todos estavam muito preocupados com a temática, ainda mais aqueles que estão implementando - além da reforma - o SAP DRC (substituto do SAP GRC NFe), uma vez que ambos os temas começam a se misturar e a SAP está lançando Notas para adequar o GRC à Reforma Tributária.

Para Produção, ainda está mantida a data de 01/01/2026.

O tempo é curto, os desafios são muitos e o governo continua firme (até o momento) sobre as datas das mudanças da Reforma.

A NT 2025.002 v 1.10, na íntegra, está disponível em <https://lnkd.in/d_B6KdKi>

entry.title: Re: Advanced Intercompany Sales & Stock Transfer in SAP S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
Hi Bernd,

thanks for your reply.

What I mean by splitting the posting is posting the internal price and the IC transfer price on the PO are posted to 2 separate GL accounts.

The EDI intercompany solution allows you to maintain the table T076I with different GL account that are linked to the condition type in the PO.

Regards,

Gordon

entry.title: Re: need help connecting Salesforce to SAP Build Apps
entry.content:
To connect your Salesforce Connected App to SAP Build Apps, follow these steps:

  1. Set Up OAuth in Salesforce – Ensure your connected app has OAuth enabled with the correct scopes.

  2. Configure SAP BTP Destination – Verify authentication settings and API endpoints in SAP BTP.

  3. Use REST API Correctly – Check if your API calls include the right headers, authentication tokens, and endpoint URLs.

  4. Test with Postman – Validate API connectivity outside SAP Build Apps to identify potential issues.

entry.title: Not Possible to use another dimension in Account Formula
entry.content:
Hello all, 

I am trying set up formula for account calculation, but I am not able to use another dimension in the formula itself. The dimension name is correct, so I am not sure what I am doing wrong. Does anyone know how to resolve the issue?

entry.title: Atualização data rejeição NFe em Homologação
entry.content:
Após muita confusão e dúvidas sobre a partir de qual data as NF-e emitidas em homologação sem as tags da Reforma Tributária serão rejeitadas, o Governo se pronunciou e publicou a atualização da NT 2025.002 v 1.10, destacando a data de 06/10/2025 para rejeição dos documentos sem as tags da reforma tributária no ambiente de homologação.
Essa atualização trará um alívio nos projetos, uma vez que todos estavam muito preocupados com a temática, ainda mais aqueles que estão implementando - além da reforma - o SAP DRC (substituto do SAP GRC NFe), uma vez que ambos os temas começam a se misturar e a SAP está lançando Notas para adequar o GRC à Reforma Tributária.

Para Produção, ainda está mantida a data de 01/01/2026.

O tempo é curto, os desafios são muitos e o governo continua firme (até o momento) sobre as datas das mudanças da Reforma.

A NT 2025.002 v 1.10, na íntegra, está disponível em <https://lnkd.in/d_B6KdKi>

entry.title: Re: Introducing the new pipeline concept in Cloud Integration
entry.content:
@jekla_ sorry saw your question only by now, the pipeline concept is not yet supported on Edge, reason is that Edge doesn't support the Partner Directory yet, this is on the roadmap planned for Q1 next year, see https://roadmaps.sap.com/board?PRODUCT=000D3A47875C1EDB98A8A910864AC24B&PRODUCT=73555000100800003553&range=CURRENT-LAST#;INNO=59346262A6261EDF94857F3CDE5957D6

Alex

entry.title: Re: How to access and modify the data in QM01 from user-e...
entry.content: absolutly nothing of that works and that's because it's not triggered the PBO of the subscreen area of Material...

entry.title: Re: receive a notification when an onboardee completes pe...
entry.content:
@user116 

Yes, the rule definition and explanation present in Onboarding implementation Guide.

Link: Creating a Business Rule to Trigger an Email Notification When the Personal Data Collection Step is Completed | SAP Help Portal

entry.title: Re: Introducing the new pipeline concept in Cloud Integration
entry.content:
@integrateIT I guess you face any other error if you can't even open an integration flow, even if you do not have JMS queues enabled, you can open an integration flow with JMS adapter, so you may open an incident ticket

Alex

entry.title: Re: What SAP roles are required for an user to access /sap/xi/engine from CPI
entry.content: If the user works from your PO system, then the credentials have been entered into the security material incorrectly.

entry.title: New SAP Readiness Check Landing Page in SAP for Me
entry.content:
Dear all,

SAP Readiness Check has evolved over the last years to become a vital planning tool for the most critical SAP transformation journeys. Currently, SAP Readiness Check includes support for nine scenarios and has processed over 85,000 analyses since its inception in 2019.

With our continuously growing number of SAP Readiness Check scenarios, we felt it was time to revamp the SAP Readiness Check cloud application landing page. We are happy to provide you with a restructured landing page in SAP for Me and SAP Cloud ALM, offering easy navigation and orientation at first glance, and being the one-stop shop for all things related to SAP Readiness Check.

The Getting Started section contains the How to Start link, which opens a guided procedure explaining the steps to create a new SAP Readiness Check analysis.

Familiarize yourself with the steps to prepare for creating your first SAP Readiness Check analysis.

The Supported Scenarios section contains links to the central guidance note for each SAP Readiness Check scenario. Each SAP Note lists the requirements to implement and run the SAP Readiness Check data collectors for the specific scenario in your system. Please read the respective SAP Note carefully before scheduling the data collection job; it contains essential hints to avoid long-running data collection jobs and other valuable information.

After the data collection jobs finish, you can create a new analysis by uploading the generated data archive to the SAP Readiness Check landing page. To do so, click Create New Analysis in the top right corner of the Analyses section.

Enter a name for the analysis, verify your customer information, and select the results archive from your local filesystem. Accept the Terms of Use and decide if you want to authorize SAP employees access to your results.

Note: With the Authorization checkbox, you grant SAP employees access to your SAP Readiness Check results. Alternatively, you can grant individual access to specific e-mail addresses later within the analysis dashboard.

Clicking Create, the application immediately processes the provided data collection archive file and produces a new analysis, accessible as an interactive dashboard. You can select the analysis from the list and start reviewing the results.

The list of analyses can be expanded by selecting the Show All button in the Analyses section, which will display all analyses your user is authorized to access. Within the analysis list, you may search for an analysis or use the filter options to limit the view to a specific scenario or analysis status. In addition, you can create a list of favorite analyses.

 New feature: Combined Scenarios

Released with the new SAP Readiness Check cloud application landing page, you can now combine analyses of different scenarios for the same system and client.

For instance, you can now update an existing SAP Readiness Check for SAP S/4HANA analysis with the data collection archive produced by SAP Readiness Check for SAP ERP Usage and Data Profiling and/or SAP Readiness Check for SAP SuccessFactors Solutions for the same system and client. Similarly, you can update an SAP Readiness Check for SAP S/4HANA Upgrades analysis with the data collection archive produced by SAP Readiness Check for SAP SuccessFactors Solutions.

To combine analyses, use the Update Analysis button within an existing SAP Readiness Check for SAP S/4HANA or SAP Readiness Check for SAP S/4HANA Upgrades analysis to add the additional scenario. The resulting combined analysis includes a tab for each scenario within the dashboard header.

 

The What’s New section of the SAP Readiness Check landing page always shows the latest blogs from the SAP Readiness Check Community to keep you up-to-date on new scenarios, features, and enhancements.

The Additional Information section provides links to the SAP Help Portal, SAP Community, learning areas, SAP Activate, related topics, and recommended training material.

We hope you enjoy the new look and feel!

entry.title: Re: Table FQM_FLOW not updated
entry.content: Note 3501295 fixed the issue. Took a while to test it because the content of field FQM_FLOW-ORIGIN_DOCUMENT_ID changed and now the PO has the suffix "-PO".

entry.title: SAP CodeJam at Gent (Belgium) - Build AI services using the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model,
entry.content:
Dear CodeJam Team,

We are a SAP Partner interested in organizing a code jam focused on building AI services for the customer and would like to request your support in making this event a success.

We experienced that the SAP CodeJam events provide a great opportunity for us developers and our customers developers to learn and develop our skills in multiple technologies, while also connecting with each other and sharing ideas.

As our previous 3 code jams with SAP were a success, we would appreciate your assistance in organizing this event, as well as connecting us with a SAP expert who can lead the CodeJam session. We now would like to organize the code jam ‘Build AI services using the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model, SAP AI Core and SAP generative AI Hub’.

We propose hosting the event on 07, 14 or 21 August 2025 at our Gent office, with an estimated number of participants of 25. 21 August is our preferred day. Other date options can off course also be discussed.

Thank you for your time and consideration. We look forward to the opportunity to work with you in organizing again a successful CodeJam event.

Sincerely,
Jasper

entry.title: Managing SFG Assembly Alternatives in Project-Wise FG WBS BOMs
entry.content:
I have created three material BOMs for semi-finished goods (SFG). I need to assign these three alternative SFGs to three different WBS finished goods (FG) materials, each belonging to a different WBS (Work Breakdown Structure) project.

To achieve this, I created three production versions (PVs) for the alternative SFGs. During the production of SFG, there is an option to change the PV.

Suppose I choose PV03 (corresponding to alternative SFG 03) and proceed with production, I am able to execute production and generate stock against WBS element.

But the challenge I face is that when I explode the WBS BOM of the FG material, it does not display the components that are subordinate to SFG 03. I want the complete list of components that were utilized in the production of the WBS BOM.

entry.title: Re: Custom task with HTML Links
entry.content:
@abrahmbhatt5 Not needed to add the link in object definition.

Best place as per my experience is to use UI template Editor Rich Text or Hyperlink elements.

The benefit of Rich text is it allows you to open the link in new tab while hyperlink element do not provide such flexibility and open the link in the same tab.

the demerit is during migration or cutover you have to redo this again at the destination instance.

Regards

Pratyush

entry.title: Re: Blog It Forward – Marssel Vilaça
entry.content: didn't know about the caricatures... that's awesome.

entry.title: Data Export API: Getting started with Bruno
entry.content:
Introduction
The Data Export Service is an open API that enables data retrieval from SAP Analytics Cloud for integration with tools like SAP Datasphere, SAP BW, SAP HANA. It has no user interface and works by retrieving data from providers (planning models). See more 

Bruno is a lightweight, open-source API client built for developers who want a fast, privacy focused, and Git-friendly way to work with APIs. Unlike traditional API tools, Bruno saves all your requests and configurations as plain files. This makes it easy to track changes, collaborate through version control, and work offline. It’s designed to be simple, efficient, and seamlessly integrate into your development workflow. 

In Bruno, requests can be sent via 2 legged (for non-browser based application access to the REST API technical user) or 3 legged (for non-browser based application access to the REST API business user) authentication through Public API Service. In order to get the most out of Bruno, an environment should be created for each tenant.
Download Bruno 

Overview

  1. Prerequisites 
  2. Steps to Set up an OAUTH Client 
  3. How to get Started in Bruno 
  4. Creating Environments with Respective Variables 
  5. How to Send External API Requests 
  6. Sending 2 Legged Requests 
  7. Sending 3 Legged Requests 
  8. Troubleshooting 

1. Prerequisites
1.1. Have an OAUTH Client set up for 2 legged Authentication.
1.2. Have an OAUTH Client set up for 3 legged Authentication.

2. Steps to Set up an OAUTH Client
2.1. Log onto the tenant.
2.2. Select Administration Menu.


SAC Homepage

2.3. Select App Integration.
Here you can find the Authorization URL and Token URL for the tenant. Click add a new OAuth Client.


App Integration

2.4. Fill out OAUTH Client details: 
For the 2 legged OAUTH Client, ensure: 
- Purpose is set to API Access. 


2 Legged OAUTH Client Configuration

For the 3 Legged OAUTH Client, ensure:
- Purpose is set to Interactive Usage.
- Set the redirect URL to - https://oauth.pstmn.io/v1/callback


3 Legged OAUTH Client Configuration

Note that secrets can only be copied during client creation. If you don't have a copy of the secret, delete the OAuth client, and then add it again to generate a new secret. Ensure that you have a copy of the client secret when creating this new OAUTH Client.

2.5. Obtain the Client ID and Client Secret. Do not share the Client Secret.
2.6. You now should have: 

  • OAuth Client ID 
  • OAuth Client Secret 
  • Authorization URL 
  • Token URL

For a more detailed step-by-step overview, see associated post:
Getting Authenticated with Data Export and Data Import APIs

3. How to get Started in Bruno

A collection in Bruno is a structured folder that contains your API requests, environment variables, and other configuration files. Each request is saved as a plain text file, allowing you to organise and manage your API workflows in a version controlled format. 

You want to first open the collection in Bruno rather than importing the .json file. By opening the collection folder instead of importing the .json file, we have source control over the collection and don't have to export and import the .json file every time a change is made. 

3.1. Select the three dots on the left. 
3.2. Select "Open Collection". 
3.3. Select the folder for the collection you want to open. 

This is what you should see: 

Opening Collection in Bruno

4. Creating Environments with Respective Variables

Now, create a template in the environments dropdown for External API Requests. 

External API requests made to your SAP Analytics Cloud tenant will have the following variables:


External API Request Template

This environment should contain values for each variable respective to your specific tenant.

5. How to Send External API Requests

Sending External API Requests - GET 2 and 3 Legged Authentication Request 

Before sending External API Requests, you have to generate an access token. This is done with a seperate. GET Request. One for 2 legged requests - "GET 2 Legged Authentication" and one for 3 legged requests - "GET 3 Legged Authentication". These GET requests generate the access token and automatically place it in the BearerToken environment variable: 


External API Request Sample Template

Note that ClientID_2 and ClientSecret_2 is for 2 legged requests, and ClientID_3 and ClientSecret_3 is for 3 legged requests. 

This is what the "GET 2 Legged Authentication" Request looks like (under the Auth tab):


GET 2 Legged Authentication Request Configuration

This is what the "GET 3 Legged Authentication" Request looks like (under the Auth tab): 


GET 3 Legged Authentication Request Configuration

To send External API Requests, there are variables present in the "External API Requests" environment that you fill in with your respective tenant information/credentials(ClientID, ClientSecret, AuthURL, CallBackURL etc). If you want to have multiple External API environments configured for different tenants, you can easily follow the same template for other tenants (with each tenant's respective credentials). These credentials are already pre-set in the different environments and that by changing the environment, the credentials for that specific environment/tenant will be populated automatically.

Ensure that you save the information once filled in. Note the AuthURL environment variable is used for the authorization URL and the access token URL, as seen in the GET 2 and 3 Legged Authentication screenshots above. 

Authorization URL: {{AuthURL}}/oauth/authorize 

Access Token URL: {{AuthURL}}/oauth/token 

The Auth tab for each 2 or 3 legged request will be set to Inherit (from Bearer Token): 


Auth tab configuration for 2 & 3 Legged Requests

6. Sending 2 Legged Requests
If you want to send 2 legged requests, locate the "2 Legged Authentication" GET Request. Generate an access token via the 2 Legged Authentication GET Request. Ensure grant type is "Client Credentials". This access token is automatically placed in the BearerToken environment variable in the "External API Requests" environment. Start sending requests.

7. Sending 3 Legged Requests
If you want to send 3 legged requests, locate the "3 Legged Authentication" GET Request. Generate an access token via the 3 Legged Authentication GET Request. Ensure grant type is "Authorization Code". This access token is automatically placed in the BearerToken environment variable in the "External API Requests" environment. Start sending requests.

Important: When switching between environments/tenants as per folder, ensure that you get a new access token. This new access token will overwrite the old value in the BearerToken environment variable. 

8. Troubleshooting
If you find that the login dialog to put in your credentials for that tenant, is not displaying when requesting a 3 legged access token, ensure that you clear the cache and delete cookies when generating a new access token:


Clearing cache and cookies

Conclusion
Bruno is a fast, Git-friendly API client that simplifies testing and managing API requests across multiple tenants. With support for 2 legged and 3 legged authentication, environment templates, and version-controlled collections, it fits seamlessly into any developer's workflow. It's an efficient tool built for modern API development.

Useful Links
Download Bruno: https://www.usebruno.com/downloads
More on Data Export Service: 
https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_ANALYTICS_CLOUD/14cac91febef464dbb1efce20e3f1613/db62fd76514b48f8b71d695360320f4a.html) 
Step-by-step overview on setting up OAUTH Clients for authentication:
https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/getting-authenticated-with-data-export-and-data-import-api-s/bc-p/14115433

BR

entry.title: Re: Joule in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition - Setup Guide

Should I create an incident in the BC-CP-MP component (following what the message says) OR create to 'CA-JOULE' ?

entry.title: Re: Changing G/L Master Data from Cash Acct. to Balance S...
entry.content:
Hello dear user,

And thank you for asking your question in the SAP Community blog.

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, once you've created a G/L account, you can’t change the account type for example, from a Cash Account to a Balance Sheet Account after it's been saved. This is mainly to protect the consistency of your financial data.

But depending on your situation, you do have a couple of options:

 

Option 1: If the G/L account hasn’t been used yet

If you’re still in your customizing tenant (like your quality system), and the G/L account hasn’t been used in any postings or configurations, you can:

  1. Open the “Manage G/L Account Master Data” app.
  2. Find the account you want to change.
  3. Delete it if possible.
  4. Then create a new G/L account with the correct account type (e.g., choose “Balance Sheet Account” instead of “Cash Account”).

Just be sure the account hasn’t been posted to or used in any config. If it has, SAP won’t let you delete it.

 

Option 2: If the account has been used

If the account already has postings or is referenced in configuration, SAP will not let you delete or change the account type. In that case:

  1. Just create a new G/L account with the correct type.
  2. Update any configurations or settings to use the new account instead of the old one.

 

I hope this will help you answer your question.

Best regards,

Jeremy

entry.title: Re: U.S Tax Reporter Year End 2024
entry.content:
Anjali,

If your desired behavior is to not have NY02 calculated with NY0F, go ahead and delete the highlighted entry from work tax area NY0F in V_T5UTW.  That should resolve your issue.

Kim

entry.title: Re: 451 TLS required, but server does not offer STARTTLS
entry.content: Any Solution  till yet?

entry.title: Re: Microsoft Teams Integration on test vs prod environme...
entry.content:
Hello Marc,

We went with the approach of enabling this in our test tenant first. Then we had a go at it and tested it. After that, we enabled the same in production. We haven't seen any concerns with it. It does what its suppose to do and works pretty well, though I would admit that it isn't used much by our sales force.  I suppose people are just too used to Outlook. 🙂

I believe every time you do a refresh of your test tenant from production and/ or after every quarterly release (I think and may not be the case; you could confirm with C4C support through a case), we need to re-enable this feature, as it gets turned off. 

Do you have any specific questions or concerns that you would like to share?

Julius

entry.title: Re: receive a notification when an onboardee completes pe...
entry.content: @Pratuysha_Kumar thank you for your response, please what to choose on the email category when trying to create the email template?

entry.title: Make Your Feedback Count! Get Involved With SAP Analytics Cloud Help Documentation Feedback Process
entry.content:
SAP Analytics Cloud is always striving to provide the best user assistance possible to its customers. To enhance our help documentation, the user assistance team will be running a pilot in which customers will be able to add comments directly to the documentation.

The main goal of this pilot is to get closer to our customers to collect feedback and improve our documentation help based on your comments.

When does the pilot start?

The pilot is now live and will span across Q3, until the end of August.

How can you add comments to the documentation?

To be able to add comments on the SAC documentation, you simply need to register on the SAP Help Portal and create an account. Customers with an SAP S-user ID will be able to sign automatically without registering with an email account.

Once that’s done, go to the SAP Analytics Cloud official documentation and start reviewing the documentation.

To add a comment:

  1. Highlight the text you want to review.
  2. Click the pen icon.

 

  1. Add your comment in the pop-up field.
  2. Click Enter.

Your comment will be acknowledged by the user assistance team.

When should you add comments?

The pilot aims at refining the SAP Analytics Cloud documentation, and we'd love to hear your feedback on any issues you find or improvements you'd like to see in help, such as:

  • Typos and misprints.
  • Imprecisions, inconsistencies or vagueness in the documentation.
  • Clarification requests.
  • Discrepancies between what the documentation describes and how the application performs.
  • Incorrect workflows.

We kindly ask you to not consider this pilot as a feature request channel. For development enhancements or feature requests, make sure to use the appropriate Customer Influence channel.

For bugs and issues you might encounter within the product, please refer to the Support Portal.

When should you expect a response?

Depending on the comment, the time needed to investigate may vary. For this reason, we kindly ask you to be patient. We assure you that the user assistance is striving to improve the documentation based on your feedback and is doing its best to respond in a timely manner.

You will be notified via email as soon as a user assistance designer responds to it and changes its status.

Because of the documentation publication schedule, your feedback will only be visible in the documentation with the Q3 release in August. 

The team will do its best to adress all of the received feedback for QRC3.  Any remaining comments the team has not had time to address will be fixed later in QRC4.

Best Regards,

The SAP Analytics Cloud User Assistance Team

entry.content: Very detailed explanation.  Thanks @elvin_baghele

entry.title: Re: How to create the transformation to the Hierarchy of...
entry.content:
Hello @-Charlie- 

unfortunately I cannot see all the details in the picture of your Transformation ... but when I see the name like "ZDS..." are you sure, that your datasource delivers all required fields for a BW transformation?

If you are not familiar with it, I would recommend you activate a standard hierarchy datasource and create a transformation into a standard infoobject like 0MATERIAL and check which data you need in the different segments...

Otherwise, it might be helpful to see how your source data looks like and how your target hierarchy is intended?

BR, Martin

entry.title: Monetization Secrets: How Top SAP Developers Earn $15K/Month From Apps
entry.content:
Complete Revenue-Generating SAP Mobile App with ABAP Backend & Flutter Frontend

Let's build a Freemium SAP Inventory Scanner App with monetization features. This app will:

  • Scan barcodes 📷
  • Fetch SAP material data 📦
  • Submit inventory updates 🔄
  • Generate revenue via ads, subscriptions, and in-app purchases 💰
  1. ABAP Backend (SAP System)
  2. Create OData Service (Transaction SEGW)

METHOD /iwbep/if_mgw_appl_srv_runtime~get_entity.

  CASE iv_entity_name.

    WHEN 'Material'.

      " Fetch SAP material details

      SELECT SINGLE matnr, maktx, meins, labst

      FROM makt

      JOIN mard ON mard~matnr = makt~matnr

      INTO CORRESPONDING FIELDS OF er_entity

      WHERE makt~matnr = iv_key_values[ 1 ]-value

      AND makt~spras = 'EN'.

     

      IF sy-subrc <> 0.

        RAISE EXCEPTION TYPE /iwbep/cx_mgw_busi_exception.

      ENDIF.

     

    WHEN 'InventoryUpdate'.

      " Process mobile inventory updates

      DATA(ls_update) = io_data_provider->read_entry_data( ).

     

      UPDATE mard SET labst = ls_update-quantity

      WHERE matnr = ls_update-material

      AND werks = ls_update-plant.

     

      IF sy-subrc = 0.

        COMMIT WORK.

        er_entity = ls_update.

      ELSE.

        ROLLBACK WORK.

        RAISE EXCEPTION TYPE /iwbep/cx_mgw_busi_exception.

      ENDIF.

  ENDCASE.

ENDMETHOD.

  1. Enable API Access in SAP
  1. Activate IWFND/MAINT_SERVICE (SAP Gateway)
  2. Whitelist mobile app IP (if needed)
  3. Set up OAuth 2.0 (Transaction SOAUTH2)
  1. Flutter Mobile App (Frontend)
  2. Setup & SAP Connection

// lib/services/sap_service.dart

class SAPService {

  final String _odataUrl = "https://your-sap-server:port/sap/opu/odata/sap/ZINVENTORY_SRV/";

 

  Future<Material> fetchMaterial(String barcode) async {

    final response = await http.get(

      Uri.parse("${_odataUrl}MaterialSet('$barcode')"),

      headers: {"Authorization": "Bearer $accessToken"},

    );

   

    if (response.statusCode == 200) {

      return Material.fromJson(jsonDecode(response.body));

    } else {

      throw Exception("Failed to fetch material");

    }

  }

 

  Future<bool> updateStock(String material, double newQty) async {

    final response = await http.post(

      Uri.parse("${_odataUrl}InventoryUpdateSet"),

      headers: {"Authorization": "Bearer $accessToken"},

      body: jsonEncode({"Material": material, "Quantity": newQty}),

    );

    return response.statusCode == 201;

  }

}

  1. Barcode Scanner & UI

// lib/screens/scan_screen.dart

class ScanScreen extends StatefulWidget {

  @override

  _ScanScreenState createState() => _ScanScreenState();

}

 

class _ScanScreenState extends State<ScanScreen> {

  final _sapService = SAPService();

  Material? _scannedMaterial;

 

  Future<void> _scanBarcode() async {

    final barcode = await BarcodeScanner.scan(); // Using flutter_barcode_scanner

    if (barcode.isEmpty) return;

 

    final material = await _sapService.fetchMaterial(barcode);

    setState(() => _scannedMaterial = material);

  }

 

  @override

  Widget build(BuildContext context) {

    return Scaffold(

      appBar: AppBar(title: Text("SAP Inventory Scanner")),

      body: Column(

        children: [

          ElevatedButton(

            onPressed: _scanBarcode,

            child: Text("Scan Barcode"),

          ),

          if (_scannedMaterial != null) _buildMaterialCard(),

          if (_isFreeUser) AdWidget(ad: _bannerAd), // Ads for free users

        ],

      ),

    );

  }

}

  1. Monetization Strategies
  2. Google AdMob Integration

// Initialize ads in main.dart

void main() async {

  WidgetsFlutterBinding.ensureInitialized();

  MobileAds.instance.initialize();

  runApp(MyApp());

}

 

// Show banner ad

final BannerAd _bannerAd = BannerAd(

  size: AdSize.banner,

  adUnitId: Platform.isAndroid

      ? 'ca-app-pub-3940256099942544/6300978111' // Test ID

      : 'ca-app-pub-3940256099942544/2934735716',

  listener: BannerAdListener(),

  request: AdRequest(),

)..load();

  1. In-App Purchases (Premium Features)

 

// lib/services/purchase_service.dart

class PurchaseService {

  static const _premiumId = 'inventory_scanner_premium';

 

  Future<bool> unlockPremium() async {

    final available = await InAppPurchase.instance.isAvailable();

    if (!available) return false;

 

    final response = await InAppPurchase.instance.buyNonConsumable(

      purchaseParam: PurchaseParam(productDetails: _premiumProduct),

    );

 

    return response.status == PurchaseStatus.purchased;

  }

}

  1. Subscription Model (Monthly SAP Access)

// Check subscription status

Future<bool> _checkSubscription() async {

  final purchases = await InAppPurchase.instance.getAvailablePurchases();

  return purchases.any((p) => p.productID == 'monthly_subscription');

}

  1. Publishing to Google Play Store
  2. Requirements

✅ Android App Bundle (.aab)
✅ Privacy Policy URL (Required for AdMob)
✅ Screenshots & Demo Video
✅ Target SAP Users in description

  1. Pricing Model

Tier

Features

Price

Free

Basic scanning, Ads

$0

Pro

No ads, Advanced reports

$4.99

Enterprise

Real-time sync, API access

$9.99/month

  1. Post-Launch Growth

📌 Promote on SAP forums (SAP Community, LinkedIn)
📌 Offer free trials for enterprise users
📌 Add more SAP modules (Sales, MM, PP)

Final Thoughts

This app connects Flutter (mobile) + ABAP (SAP) with multiple revenue streams.

 

 

 The Opportunity

Imagine SAP users pulling out their phones to:
✅ Scan barcodes and instantly fetch material details from SAP
✅ Update inventory in real-time
✅ Access reports on the go

entry.title: Attributes-to-Sap for Excel
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

In our excel documents that we upload to the ECTR system, can we match some data from defined fixed cells in Excel as characteristics and pull them to ECTR, that is, SAP. Just like we pull CATIA parameters to ECTR by mapping, I need to pull some standard data in Excel instantly.

Thank you for your support

Nazif Kara

entry.title: Re: Include Batch expiration in Material Requirement Planning
entry.content:
Hello AshishMondalSCMPPQM,

 

Thank you a lot by this article. I have a question: What happen when you are using PPDS, is necessary apply this steps. only those materials that doesn´t run production planning PPDS? and what happen with those material that only use MRP live in the center distribution?

I appreciate your prompt response regarding this.

Best reg

entry.title: Re: lead qualification how to auto set based on date
entry.content:
Hello Sunil,

C4C workflows would be the ideal way to do this. However, since there is no provision in the conditions in the workflow to check the current date with an expected date, we are at a loss. However you could try a workaround and see if this works.

1. Create a custom KUT field of type date and call it currentdate

2. Create a workflow rule called 'set current date' on every save and in the actions, choose field update and set the value of this custom date field to the system date using the update type = calculated. 

3. Create 3 custom KUT fields Sales_in_30_date, Sales_in_60_date and Sales_in_90_date. 

3. Now create a workflow 'set sales in 30' on every save and in the actions, choose field update and set the value of Sales_in_30_date = currentdate + 30 using the update type formula. 

4. Similarly create 2 more workflows to set Sales_in_60_date and Sales_in_90_date fields.

5. Last you create one workflow of type on every save and check if the currentdate is > Sales_in_90_date then set qualification to Cold. OR if currentdate is < Sales_in_90_date AND great than Sales_in_60_date then set qualification to Warm. etc. 

(please check which logic works; the above is just a sample logic which you will have to check and set)

The only challenge I see with the above is that the order in which the workflows are called. I'm not sure about that. You could do some investigation in that. If they are executed in some order, then the above could work. But if they are executed all at once, this may not work. But I think its worth a shot. 

Last resort, is to achieve this through sdk. On save of the lead, you check the dates and set the qualification accordingly. 

Julius

entry.title: Perform Manual Reconciliation
entry.content:
Hello!

During the testing of J78 test script we have arrived to the step 4.6.2 Perform Manual Reconciliation. When we open we only see one bank account an has a status of 'not applicable'. Why is that? What did we miss?

We have the neccessarry configurations. We have bank statements uploaded. 

Thanks!

Or where can business personnel query the archived resale and claim records?

entry.title: Re: Unlocking the Power of SAP Central Business Configuration for Cloud Solutions through Scoping
entry.content:
Hello,

I would have 2 questions please:

1. Is there an overview available where I can see the list of Scope Items and their contents, like: GL Accounts, Reports, Account Groups, Fiori Apps etc? So basically a list of all Scope Items and their related content. I could not find such a file until now respectively an overview somewhere.

 

2. Some Scope Items mentioned in the YCOA COA File for Finance, column 8, cannot be found in the Availability Dependencies List. Why is that?

For example: J74 or J76 in the YCOA column N does not appear in the Availabilities Dependencies List. Where can I find a list of all possible Scope Items across all LoBs and Industries, in form of an Excel file?

 

Thank you!

Daniel Nicula

entry.title: Re: Improving my AdT Report template - part 3
entry.content:
Somtimes I want a very SIMPLE report template; it's so view lines, it's noch even an own blog:

CLASS lcl_report DEFINITION.
  PUBLIC SECTION.
    METHODS: main.
ENDCLASS.

CLASS lcl_report IMPLEMENTATION.
  METHOD main.
    "...code here:
  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.

START-OF-SELECTION.
  NEW lcl_report( )->main( ).

entry.title: Re: Promotion Functionality in SuccessFactors Compensation
entry.content:
1.  You can have different publishing dates per country but you need to have custom fields configured in the template and then create the publishing instructions in the XML.

2.  There is not a standard report that shows who has been promoted but you can create an ad-hoc report based on the compensation template to extract the information you need

3.  Creating a different budget is handled just like any other budget.   You need a field to store the allocation (standard or custom) and then another field to store the spend (usually the promotion field)

4  Yes, you can use the extra (Adjustment) field in addition and configure the label as needed.   It is not connected to the promotion functionality

5.  I am not sure what you are trying to do in this question, however, before the promotion the now position needs to exist in the system.  In addition, if the new position will have direct reports, you are not going to be able to complete transfers so that the new employees report to the new manager in this transaction.  This needs to happen outside the compensation template in a separate activity.

David Somelofske

Principal Business Process Consultant

entry.title: Re: IAS ERROR READ & REASYNC JOB
entry.content:
Hi Yogananda! Thank you for your input.

I've reviewed the properties of both the source and destination systems, but certificate authentication has been implemented in both cases.

I'm sharing supporting images.

entry.title: Re: Rework required for Fiori apps exposed in SAP Workzon...
entry.content:
Thanks for the clarification. Your assumption might not be correct. Client side can only connect with the remote source by the usage of HTTP APIs, and in that sense CORS or Cross-Site Scripting could be an issue (which could happen in the cloud but on on premise). If that is the case I'd expect the OpenText solution has some sort of Admin/configuration panel to address that.

I saw something like this in 3051989 - "This content cannot be displayed in a frame" error if Business Workspace is opened from SAP for OpenText Extended ECM - SAP for Me) but I wonder if this is part of the same solution you are using (it is under component XX-PART-OPT-ECM-CLD)

To solve this problem:

  1. open Content Server admin page
  2. go to "Configure Security Parameters"
  3. unselect the option: "Frame Embedding: Prevent request handlers from being embedded in external frames"

There are different means by exposing an app in WorkZone. The app could be deployed in BTP or in the on-premise server and exposed via Content Federation (aka Content Exposure). WorkZone uses an iframe to embed some apps, which could also indicate a reason for not reaching the server.

That iFrame is the reason some HTTPs calls when done in SAPUI5 do not work in WorkZone unless you adapt the source code a little bit - but this is not always the case.

The easier way of understanding how this communication works, I highly suggest you doing it is my tracing the HTTP calls while you use the Fiori app in the browser by using the browser Developer Tools (any Fiori/front end developer can help you to do this in case you need support). In Chrome or Edge, just press F12 while having the app opened an check the "Network" tab.

I suggest to repeat this process both when executing the app in the local network and in work zone. If the call in workzone really fails, it should be even easier to identify in the Network tab as it will return some 4xx or 5xx error (despite being read).

Please attach those snapshots here. You can check the Headers >> General section for that request to see the URL being called and compare both scenarios.

The "Console" tab is also useful in case of CORS, Cross-Site Scripting issues.

entry.title: Re: supplier invoice workflow
entry.content: Hi Fernanda, all links are either obsolete or we as partners are not authorized. Can you please update them?

entry.title: Access to Management Bank – Cash Management Application
entry.content:
Which Scope item is required to access the Management Bank – Cash Management application.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Access to Management Bank – Cash Management Application
entry.content: Scope item BFA "Basic Bank Account Management" will be required for the App Manage Bank - Cash Management. https://me.sap.com/processnavigator/SolP/BFA

entry.title: Re: Perform Manual Reconciliation
entry.content:
Hello dear user,

And thank you for asking your question in the SAP Community blog.

If in step 4.6.2 Perform Manual Reconciliation you only see one bank account with status "Not Applicable", it usually means:

What likely went wrong :

  • No open items to reconcile – The bank statement may have already cleared everything automatically.
  • No bank statement uploaded or processed – Manual reconciliation needs posted statement items.
  • Bank GL account not set as open item managed – Reconciliation requires this setting.
  • Wrong filters – Could be using the wrong company code or account filter.

What to check :

  • Use Manage Bank Statements to confirm a statement was uploaded and items are not cleared.
  • Use Display Journal Entries to see if postings hit the bank GL.
  • Use Manage G/L Account Master Data to check that the GL is open item managed.
  • Review filters in Perform Manual Reconciliation.

I hope this will help you answer your question.

Best regards,

Jeremy

entry.title: Issue with User Visibility in People Picker on Manage Flexible Workflow Application
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

We are currently in the process of configuring a workflow within the Manage Flexible Workflow application. However, we are encountering an issue where no users are visible in the People Picker field. Despite ensuring that all required prerequisites as per the KBA mentioned below have been met, we are still unable to see any users listed.

0002824240 - No users available in People picker of Manage workflow application

0002869055 - No users available in Manage Workflows app - SAP S/4HANA

We have followed the standard setup guidelines and checked the user assignments, but the problem persists. Could anyone from the community suggest what could be causing this issue or any troubleshooting steps we might have missed?

entry.title: Goods Movement Analysis : Consumption Quantity wrong
entry.content:
Hi 

I'm looking for into  Goods Movement Analysis and I don't understand why SAP does not considered as Consumption Quantity the movement 601 with Special Stock Type = E.

They come from MTO orders . 

Example 

Material   Mov      Stock type   Qty

AAAAA     601                             10

AAAAA     601               E            5

In my opinion the total consumption must be 15 and not 10 ( ad per SAP)

Best regards

entry.title: Re: Ensure License Compliance
entry.content:
Hi Jacek,

I don't know what happened but somehow the Consumption node and Show Report action started to show again after Reset. I added the consumption authority to the backofficeadministrator role which is a member of the admingroup. My has admingroup role but it was not working until I assign the backofficeadministrator role. It looks like authority are not searched recursively through roles.

Pascal

entry.title: Re: Responsible Cost Center in WBS element and Project
entry.content:
Hello dear user,

And thank you for asking your question in the SAP Community blog.

this is a common point of confusion in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition regarding the role of the Responsible Cost Center on WBS Elements/Projects and settlement strategy configuration.

 

1. Responsible Cost Center in Project/WBS

  • The Responsible Cost Center is mandatory because it determines who “owns” or manages the project/WBS from a controlling perspective.
  • This cost center controls where costs are posted and also drives the company code determination for the project, since cost centers are assigned to a controlling area and company code.
  • It helps ensure proper cost assignment and internal controlling, for example in cost planning, budget monitoring, and settlements.

 

2. Company Code Determination from Cost Center

  • The system derives the Company Code for the project based on the company code assigned to the responsible cost center.
  • This is standard behavior, because cost centers belong to a controlling area, which belongs to a company code, and projects must be assigned to exactly one company code.

 

3. Settlement Strategy & Settlement Rule in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

  • In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, you cannot manually create or change settlement strategies like in SAP ECC or Private Cloud versions.
  • The settlement strategy is predefined by SAP standard for projects and WBS elements.
  • You do not select settlement strategies manually; instead, the system automatically uses the default settlement rule created during WBS or project creation.

 

4. Where is the Settlement Strategy Managed in Public Cloud?

  • The settlement rules and strategies are typically predefined and managed by SAP.
  • The Responsible Cost Center in WBS drives automatic creation of a settlement receiver in the settlement rule, generally pointing to the cost center or the internal order assigned.
  • You can review settlement rules in the project system, but modifications are limited or restricted in Public Cloud.
  • In some cases, you can configure settlement profiles or parameters via SAP Best Practice Configuration in the Manage Your Solution app or via SAP Fiori apps, but direct creation of settlement strategies as in ECC is not possible.
  • CBC (Central Business Configuration) manages broad scope items and business configuration but does not allow creating custom settlement strategies for projects.

 

I hope this will help you answer your question.

Best regards,

Jeremy

entry.title: Re: BTP Integration Suite Error
entry.content: Tried the above and still cannot create an Integration Flow Artifact within the Package

entry.title: Re: Max retry and duration of retry by CIG / Spend Manage...
entry.content:
CIG will try to resend the ddocument failed, 3 times (once per hour) afte the initial failure.
In any case, when the document failed and has enter "RETRY" or "FAILED", the CIG Admin can come back and select all the failed transaction and click on "Reprocess >> Oubound" that will resend all the documents again.
Note that CIG keeps the transaction of the last 30 days.

entry.title: MDG Filter Search Help of Characteristic Value
entry.content:
Hello,

In SAP MDG on an S/4 HANA 2023 system, is it possible to filter the search help of a characteristic in "Lean Classification" management?

For example, if in the list of characteristics there is MDM_MOTHER_CODE with a specific value, I need to filter the values for the characteristic MDM_DM_CALIBRO based on the value of the MDM_MOTHER_CODE.

Where do I need to make changes?

Thank you

entry.title: Re: My BTP Trail account Subscription failed for Integrat...
entry.content:
I had this occur for me also today.  Delete the failed Integration Suite installation and try again.  Also make sure that your login has not expired.  I found that I had timed out of my trial account and had to login again as well.  After that, it successfully installed the Integration Suite.

I did the re-install of Integration Suite because when going to Design -> Integrations and APIs -> Package, I still cannot add and Integration Flow to the Package.  This is still not solved and a number of people have reported this and are stuck.  Hoping SAP will look at their CPI Trial Account and figure out what is causing this problems, so we can continue learning about Integration Flows.

entry.title: Fiori Elements List Report: How to enable the export to pdf option using BAS?
entry.content: Hello. Im currently creating a full stack CAP application in the BAS environment. When I create my list report using fiori elements, the only option I get to export my data is an XLSX file. Even when I choose the "export as" option, the only option given is the excel file. Is there a configuration to enable the PDF Export? Because I was looking at the official documentation but couldn't find any practical example for my development environment. Thanks in advance.
